title: BCMA Claim Set Expert Review Packet v0

target_artifacts.review_goal: Review claim scope, evidence level, measurement assumptions, safety language, and unsupported inference.
Review whether gaps and next actions are the right public work queue for the current evidence.
Review whether the measurement-context fields are sufficient before stronger claims are attempted.
Review whether coverage gaps are framed as artifact gaps rather than mechanism rankings.

review_status_boundary: The target artifacts are source-checked public organization artifacts.
This packet does not make the target artifacts expert-reviewed.
Expert-reviewed status requires completed comments from qualified reviewers and a follow-up artifact update.

review_questions: Is the claim limited to public research extraction fields rather than patient-level interpretation?
Are assay method, specimen source, timing, paired-measurement context, and denominator fields sufficient before the claim is strengthened?
Should any wording be weakened before this claim is used in an educational layer?
required_reviewer_roles: multiple myeloma clinician or translational scientist
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Confirm or revise the claim boundary and minimum measurement fields before any public education summary uses this claim.

review_questions: Is the TNFRSF17 wording properly limited to a trackable source-specific signal?
What assay, specimen, and paired-sample context is required before any stronger biomarker claim?
Does the claim avoid implying a mechanism frequency or product-specific rate?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Review whether the biomarker language should stay source-specific or be weakened further.

review_questions: Does the claim keep target biology separate from clinical efficacy?
Does the extraction preserve normal-tissue reactivity and safety context without claiming clinical safety?
Is the trial-registry boundary clear enough for public readers?
required_reviewer_roles: CAR T or cell-therapy translational scientist
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Confirm that SEMA4A remains an alternate-target research signal only until clinical and safety evidence supports stronger language.

review_questions: Does the claim appropriately prevent antigen-escape over-weighting without ranking mechanisms?
Which non-antigen-loss buckets need first or second source extraction before comparison?
Does the current language avoid implying that any resistance-overcoming strategy improves survival?
required_reviewer_roles: multiple myeloma clinician or translational scientist
CAR T immune-state or tumor-microenvironment reviewer
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Review whether the multifactorial framing is accurate, readable, and appropriately bounded.

review_questions: Would a patient, advocate, or builder mistake any claim for medical guidance?
Are source-checked, expert-review-needed, and expert-reviewed statuses visually and textually distinct?
Are all unsupported cure, treatment, trial, diagnostic, frequency, and safety inferences blocked?
required_reviewer_roles: multiple myeloma clinician or translational scientist
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Review public language before any patient-facing explainer or evidence-strength summary is created.
safety_boundary: Not a treatment or trial recommendation.

completion_gate: At least one qualified reviewer must complete each review item before any target artifact is labeled expert-reviewed.
Reviewer comments must be mapped back to claim IDs, gap IDs, source IDs, measurement term IDs, and extraction record IDs.
Any claim-strength increase requires explicit reviewer support and a follow-up pull request.
Any incomplete evidence should weaken or clarify public wording rather than strengthen it.
interpretation_boundary: This packet is review infrastructure, not completed expert review.
This packet is not medical advice, diagnostic guidance, treatment guidance, trial guidance, or a cure claim.
This packet does not make patient-specific recommendations.
This packet does not upgrade source-checked claims to expert-reviewed claims.

review_notes: Separates review readiness from completed expert review.
Updated to include the disease-context extraction record added for public issue #16.
Updated to include Di Meo 2025 target-linked phenotype extraction for plasma-cell identity second-source coverage.
Updated to include NCI PDQ disease-context extraction for second-source context-field coverage.
